raw_response: {"rating": -0.4, "lean": "Center", "explanation": "This is a news report. The author’s own prose is largely neutral and descriptive, with slight left-lean shading in a few word choices and framings, but no sustained ideological argument. The limited directional cues do not rise above Center, yielding a small Lean Left tint within the Center band. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author frames local authority finances with urgency — EHCP claims have “soared” and councils are “forced to run budget deficits… estimated to reach a collective £5bn” — crisis framing that is not distinctly ideological but sets a reform-urgency tone; (2) The description of policy discussions says local authorities are lobbying for “more control over costs by limiting parental rights to appeals,” a phrasing that emphasizes a reduction of “rights” rather than administrative streamlining, subtly aligning with left-lean sensitivities toward protections for individuals; (3) The piece repeats the aim of making mainstream schools “more inclusive” in the author’s narration (not just quotes), adopting value-positive terminology commonly associated with left-lean education discourse; (4) Beyond these instances, the remaining authorial text is straightforward event and process description (white paper preparation, a call for evidence, who will lead it), with no balancing or oppositional editorializing, keeping overall lean minimal."}
